topic: Performance Analysis and Performance Development Programme
subject: pe

items.q: What does the 'S' in SMART targets stand for?
items.why: Students invent words; SMART = Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, Time-bound.

items.correct: Gradually increasing training demands so the body keeps adapting
items.why: Students think harder is always better; overload must be gradual and progressive to be effective and safe.

items.q: What is the purpose of notational analysis?
items.correct: To collect objective data on performance
items.why: Students think analysis is subjective; notational analysis records measurable events for objective feedback.

items.correct: Objective data about performance, e.g. number of successful passes
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Objective data about performance, e.g. number of successful passes. “A random opinion only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In the FITT principle, what does 'T' (Type) refer to?
items.correct: The kind of training used (e.g. interval, continuous)
items.why: Correct answer: The kind of training used (e.g. interval, continuous). “The time of day only” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
